title: Earnings: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Campus reported net income of $1,712 as of March 31, 2026. Interest income totaled $10,792 against interest expense of $2,989; the difference between these two figures was the main source of this period's earnings.

Non-interest income totaled $2,608 and represented a modest piece of overall earnings. The majority comes from fee income at $1380 and other non-interest income at $19.

Non-interest expense totaled $8,359 and appeared proportionate to the institution's size for this reporting period. Credit loss expense accounts for $340 followed by operating fees (examina...) at $262; expenses were spread across several categories rather than concentrated in one area.

Overall Position:
The credit union maintained profitability through a healthy gap between interest income and interest expense, while keeping non-interest costs under control. Sustaining profitability will depend on maintaining the current spread or managing the pace of expense growth.
